title: The Camp Dog
story_html:
Leo felt lonely on his first night at summer camp. He did not know anyone, and his cabin felt too quiet. The next morning, he decided to explore with a paper map of the campground, hoping to find something fun to do.

Near the north side of the lake, Leo heard a soft sound in the bushes. He walked closer, feeling curious, and found a small, dirty dog hiding under a bush. The dog looked tired, as if it had been trying to survive alone in the woods for days. Leo felt sorry for it and knew he wanted to help.

He picked up the dog gently and carried it back to his cabin. The head counselor said Leo could keep it for the rest of the week if he could take care of it. Leo felt this was an incredible chance. He gave the dog a bath, fed it, and made it a comfortable bed from an old blanket.

The little dog quickly became loyal to Leo, following him everywhere. It seemed to protect him too, barking softly whenever a stranger came near their tent. Kids from every cabin started to gather around the tent each evening just to see the pet and smile at its silly tricks.

Leo's friends suggested a fun idea: they would support Leo by giving the dog a special title. They chose "Trail Hero," since the dog had helped everyone feel more connected and closer to nature.

By the end of the week, Leo could not imagine leaving the dog behind. His parents agreed to let him bring it home on the flight, and promised they would return next summer with their new best friend. As Leo packed, he made sure one small item stayed close by his side — the dog's favorite blanket, deep inside his backpack, ready for the whole trip home.

lonely.def: Feeling sad because you are alone or have no friends.

smile.def: To make a happy expression with your mouth.

return.def: To give something back, or to go back to a place.

map.def: A picture that shows a place.

comfortable.def: Feeling relaxed, with no pain or worry.

loyal.def: Always supporting and being faithful to someone.

protect.def: To keep someone or something safe.

survive.def: To continue to live through a difficult situation.

gather.def: To come together in a group.

rest.def: A period of relaxing after work or effort.

pet.def: An animal kept at home for company.

deep.def: Going down a long way; a slow, full breath.

suggest.def: To offer an idea or a plan.

silly.def: Funny in a foolish or playful way.

camp.def: A place where people stay outside.

nature.def: Everything in the world not made by people.

title.def: The name of a book, story, or piece of work.

curious.def: Wanting to know or learn about something.

chance.def: A time when something might happen.

special.def: Not ordinary; different in a good way.

item.def: A single thing on a list or in a group.

questions.qtext: In "Leo felt lonely on his first night at summer camp. He did not know anyone, and his cabin felt too quiet," what does lonely mean?
options: sad from being alone with no friends around
questions.explain: B — Leo did not know anyone and his cabin felt too quiet, which shows he felt sad and alone.

questions.qtext: In "The dog looked tired, as if it had been trying to survive alone in the woods for days," what does survive mean?
options: to play happily with other animals
to run away and hide forever
to stay alive through a hard situation
to sleep for a very long time
questions.explain: C — the dog was tired and alone in the woods, struggling to stay alive on its own.

questions.qtext: In "It seemed to protect him too, barking softly whenever a stranger came near their tent," what does protect mean?
options: to keep someone safe from danger
to teach someone a new trick
questions.explain: A — the dog barked at strangers near the tent, which is a way of keeping Leo safe.

questions.qtext: Why did the head counselor let Leo keep the dog for the rest of the week?
options: Because the dog belonged to another camper
Because Leo found the dog and agreed to take care of it
Because the counselor did not like dogs
Because Leo paid money for the dog
questions.explain: B — Leo found the tired, dirty dog and the counselor agreed he could keep it if he took care of it.

questions.qtext: How did Leo and the other campers feel about the dog by the end of the story?
options: They were scared of it and stayed away
They forgot about it after a few days
They loved it, gave it a special title, and wanted it to come home with Leo
They decided to leave it behind in the woods
questions.explain: C — the campers gathered around the dog, gave it the title "Trail Hero," and Leo's parents let him bring it home.
